title: VS Code extension does ONE job: Why + Capture — dashboards, console, review GUI, graph, codelens, diagnostics deleted
context: The v0.8.0 extension had grown to 20 commands, 4 webviews (home/console/graph/stats), an activity-bar tree, codelens, hover, diagnostics, decorations, and 2 status bars. The owner could no longer say what the extension does; complexity was making his own flagship surface no fun to use. Simplification options considered: guard-only tripwire, keep-all-behind-one-home-view, or cut to the memory loop.
decision: The extension keeps exactly the memory loop and nothing else. READ: 'Hunch: Why is this?' (file brief + symbol section when the cursor is on a symbol with signal), symbol hover, and a status-bar invariant counter. WRITE: one 'Hunch: Capture…' picker (decision via the hunch mcp write path, invariant/bug via the CLI). Plus 'Hunch: Search memory' and the three invisible language-model tools. Everything else — home, console, stats webview, component graph, review-queue GUI, changeGate, codelens, diagnostics, decorations, the activity-bar tree — is deleted, not hidden; draft triage lives in the CLI (hunch review). Source went 16 files/3776 lines to 7 files/~1600 lines; v0.9.0. Litmus for future additions: if the surface can't be explained in one sentence, it doesn't go in the extension.
consequences: Command palette shows 3 Hunch commands instead of 20.
Draft review, drift, conformance, and stats are CLI/MCP-only again.
The extension remains a pure reader; all writes stay delegated (CLI or hunch mcp).
Re-adding a surface requires superseding this decision, not just a PR.
alternatives_rejected: Guard-only tripwire extension (diagnostics + catches counter) — rejected: drops the write half of the loop, and capture-in-the-editor is the moat behavior.
Keep all features behind a single Home entry point — rejected: complexity survives one click down; the confusion returns.
Keep codelens/diagnostics/decorations as ambient signal — rejected: three overlapping ambient channels for the same facts; hover + status bar carry the same signal quietly.
